topStories.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Vows To Continue Support For Resistance Against Israel, US
topStories.description: In a message to Hezbollah's Sheikh Naim Qassem, Khamenei appreciated the "emotion, love and loyalty" shown by Hezbollah fighters.

topStories.title: Cameo partners with TikTok to boost popularity
topStories.description: Cameo launched a new TikTok integration that allows U.S. creators to offer personalized videos through the app.

topStories.title: Trump to give primetime address on war as questions swirl over his next move
topStories.description: The US president finds himself under growing pressure at home to avoid a protracted conflict.

topStories.title: Quote of the Day: Leonardo da Vinci on art and knowledge — ‘Where the spirit does not work with the hand…'
topStories.description: Leonardo da Vinci’s timeless words connect art with science, reminding us that creativity thrives when passion, observation and purpose come together.

topStories.title: US Supreme Court signals skepticism over Trump’s birthright citizenship order
topStories.description: Trump remained for just over an hour, listening as Solicitor General D. John Sauer defended the administration’s position before departing shortly after opposing counsel began presenting.

topStories.title: What do Trump's latest comments on leaving Nato mean for the alliance?
topStories.description: Trump has criticised Nato members for showing a lack of support for US objectives in Iran.

topStories.title: Who is Jeremy Hansen? This Artemis II astronaut is first Canadian ever to fly around the Moon
topStories.description: Artemis II launch: Canada will script history when Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Jeremy Hansen becomes the first Canadian – and first non-American – to ever participate in a lunar mission.

topStories.title: Iran denies Trump’s ceasefire claim as ‘false and baseless’ amid escalating war rhetoric
topStories.description: The denial came hours after Trump claimed on Truth Social that Iran had sought a ceasefire, adding that the US would only consider it if the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

topStories.title: Property linked to Memphis rapper Pooh Shiesty searched by FBI
topStories.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) agents on Wednesday (local time) carried out a search warrant at a home linked to Memphis rapper Pooh Shiesty.

dailyNews.title: Exclusion from rolls doesn’t repeal voting rights forever, says Supreme Court
dailyNews.description: Court asks Election Commission tribunals to ensure fair adjudication of electoral roll exclusions during Special Intensive Revision

dailyNews.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Could Launch Largest-Ever Stock Market Listing: Report
dailyNews.description: The Starbase, Texas-headquartered firm could seek to raise more than $50 billion in the IPO, handily surpassing the 2019 flotation of Saudi Aramco, which remains the largest IPO on record.

dailyNews.title: North Korea-linked hackers breach Axios software to target US firms; crypto theft feared: Report
dailyNews.description: Suspected North Korean hackers breached the open-source Axios package, pushing malicious updates to many US firms. Experts warn of a prolonged campaign to steal crypto funds. Dozens of devices are already compromised, with wider impact expected across industries.

dailyNews.description: Artemis II launch: Anticipation is at its peak for the mission, which will send four astronauts on a ten day trip around the Moon and back, marking the first time humans have travelled to the Moon time since the Apollo missions

dailyNews.title: 'Iran asked for ceasefire': Trump makes bold claim, says ‘will consider when…’ as conflict with Tehran rages
dailyNews.description: US' conflict with Iran, where the Strait of Hormuz has emerged as a central point, is over a month old. UAE is reportedly preparing to help United States and other allies open the key waterway by force, reports stated

dailyNews.title: Nearly 1,200 Indians, mostly students, have returned from Iran: Officials
dailyNews.description: About 9,000 Indians, including students and businesspeople, were in Iran when military strikes by Israel and the US on February 28 triggered the conflict

dailyNews.title: WWE NXT star Tony D'Angelo warns title contenders he's on a 'path of destruction'
dailyNews.description: Tony D'Angelo will challenge for Joe Hendry's NXT Championship on Saturday night at Stand & Deliver along with Ricky Saints and Ethan Page.

dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court weighs whether the 14th Amendment grants citizenship to U.S.-born children of undocumented immigrants, with a ruling potentially expected by June.

dailyNews.title: 'Wheel of Fortune' star Pat Sajak gives rare glimpse into retirement in viral social media spotlight
dailyNews.description: Pat Sajak and daughter Maggie are going viral with playful social media videos, giving fans a fun look at the former "Wheel of Fortune" host's retirement life.

dailyNews.title: One of the NFL's first female officials sues the league for gender discrimination after being fired
dailyNews.description: Robin DeLorenzo, one of the NFL's first female officials, alleges gender discrimination and humiliation in a new lawsuit after being fired in 2025.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls for second 'big, beautiful bill' to fund ICE on his desk by June 1
dailyNews.description: Trump pushes Republicans to use budget reconciliation to fund ICE and border agents, setting a June 1 deadline to end the 47-day DHS shutdown.

dailyNews.title: Poll position: Where Trump stands among Americans as he faces the nation in primetime
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump's approval ratings have edged down in recent weeks, sinking further underwater as he faces the nation on Wednesday night to update Americans on the Iran war.

dailyNews.title: Inside Supreme Court: How Trump heard birthright citizenship arguments
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump made history attending Supreme Court arguments on birthright citizenship, where justices voiced strong skepticism over his executive order.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Scales Back Plan to Dismantle Consumer Protection Bureau
dailyNews.description: A new filing asks a federal court to allow the White House to dismiss much of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s remaining work force, but not close the bureau entirely.

dailyNews.title: Trump Attends Supreme Court Oral Arguments in a Presidential First
dailyNews.description: President Trump’s presence in the court puts him face to face with justices whom he has tried to bully and intimidate.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Europe Is On Its Own in Securing the Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Threatening to pull out of NATO, President Trump portrayed the alliance as a “paper tiger” and said Europe was on its own in trying to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: The Birthright Con
dailyNews.description: Trump wants to find out just how weak this Supreme Court is.

dailyNews.title: Padma Lakshmi: The Decision That Would Create a Permanent American Underclass
dailyNews.description: Trump wants to take away a fundamental American promise.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court casts doubt on Trump's order to end birthright citizenship
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday appeared open to invalidating President Trump's executive order that would end birthright citizenship.

dailyNews.title: Iran announces new strikes on U.S. targets hours ahead of Trump speech
dailyNews.description: Trump says he expects the U.S. war with Iran to end within several weeks despite unrelenting attacks from both sides and Iran's iron grip on the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Judge rules Trump cutting off funding for NPR and PBS is unconstitutional
dailyNews.description: U.S. District Judge Randolph Moss blocked the Trump administration from enforcing provisions of his executive order that directed federal agencies to cut off funding to NPR and PBS.

dailyNews.title: President Trump's 2026 State of the Union Address and the Democratic response
dailyNews.description: President Trump delivers the longest State of the Union address in recent history, followed by the Democratic response from Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger.
